rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF CARLOS NELSON MONTOYA JR.
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting, Fees
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 9/3/25. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: (1) Notice must have attached to it the procedures for how to make a remote appearance by Zoom and CourtCall, as well as remote public access, as set out at https://sf.courts.ca.gov/divisions/probate-court. [LRSF 14.1 C.6.] (2) When an accounting is presented, original bank or brokerage statements must be filed to verify the ending balance(s) shown in the accounting. None appears to have been filed. [LRSF 14.34 D.] See CRC 7.575(b) for computer-generated printouts. (3) The statutory fee is miscalculated. Losses on sale are subtracted. (4) Any amount paid to a third party to prepare the accounting must be deducted from the personal representative's statutory compensation. (5) Refunds that were for amounts paid out of the estate or advances to the estate for which someone is to be reimbursed are not receipts includable in the fee base. (6) Attorney costs must clearly indicate what was filed in order for the Court to determine the accuracy of the e-filing costs. (What Court Admin fee?) (7) The Court will not ordinarily order fees to be paid that are yet to be determined; a reserve amount should be requested from which closing costs can be paid. (8) A petition for final distribution must state specifically how the estate is to be distributed by showing the distributee's name and description of the property, including the complete description of each individual security, and the net amount of cash (as of a date certain) distributable to each beneficiary. [CRC 7.651; LRSF 14.35 I.6.] (9) A petition for final distribution must state specifically how the estate is to be distributed by showing the distributee's name and description of the property, including the complete description of each individual security, and the net amount of cash (as of a date certain) distributable to each beneficiary. [CRC 7.651; LRSF 14.35 I.6.] Please provide a revised proposed order and courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues to the assigned examiner by 8/20/25. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B. NB: In order for this Court to track closed matters, orders to terminate and distribute an estate must begin with the following language: "Order for Final Distribution." [LRSF 14.35 I.6.b.]

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF CLARENCE I. WILLIAMS
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 10/27/25. Examiner's Notes with the following issues sent to attorney: 1) Rejection of creditor's claim filed on 4/29/25 was noticed on 6/30/25, and 90 days must pass prior to the estate closing. Please file a verified supplemental declaration after the date to take action on the claim has passed and allege whether any such action was taken. 2) Par. 12 of the petition indicates that petitioner requests costs in the amount of $846.50; however, the prayer of the petition does not include this. An amendment to the petition will be needed if still being requested. 3) Petitioner alleges that there is over $624K in one bank. Proof of full FDIC insurance must be filed prior to this estate closing. 4) Total to be distributed to the trust is alleged to be $592,612.85. Please provide the calculation, beginning with the stated assets on hand ($624,583.33).

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF SHAUNA L. WOODS
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR SPOUSAL PROPERTY
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 8/4/2025 for the following issues: (1) Attachment 7 merely states that both spouses contributed equally to the down payment and mortgage payments. However, it does not establish that the property in question is considered community property, nor does it provide other necessary information required by Local Rule 14.26.A. The allegations should include details about the manner and timing of the property's acquisition, any property interests that each spouse owned at the time of marriage, whether the funds used to acquire the property came from gifts or inheritances, and other relevant information. A verified supplement to be filed one week before the hearing date. (2) Notice of Directions for Remote appearance and Access must be given. LRSF 14.1.C. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10.B.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF LUCINDA JANE SIEGEL
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
rulings.Rulings: Hearing to admit will. No appearance required. Status hearing on filing of petition for final distribution or petition for approval on report of status of administration will be set for 08/17/2026.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF EUNICE MARTHA LEU
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
rulings.Rulings: Grant without hearing. No appearance required. Status Hearing: 8/31/2026. The personal representative shall file either a petition for an order for final distribution or a petition for approval of report of status of administration pursuant to Prob. Code sec.12200 et seq.
